Replay 912130 turn 49: Curse then Earthquake uses sleeping Snorlax's wake window to improve the endgame route; immediate Earthquake is active but can fail to convert before Snorlax wakes.

Replay 912130 turn 49: switching to Zapdos preserves the final cleaner but gives sleeping Snorlax a chance to wake without forced progress; Curse converts the current sleep window while Zapdos remains available after the trade.

Holdout trajectory from existing high-confidence labels: Surf is the best stay-in attack, but Houndoom is faster and Crunch KOs Slowbro before Surf matters, so the switch-preserve plan dominates the attack-now plan.

Existing labels identify switch Piloswine as the missing best plan: Surf is weak chip and Thunder Wave is only maybe acceptable if Slowking survives, while Piloswine preserves Slowking and threatens back.
Existing labels identify switch Piloswine as the missing best plan: Surf does not solve Electric pressure and Rest spends a turn while Ampharos keeps initiative.
Existing labels identify switch Piloswine as the missing best plan: Thunder Wave is a calculated risk if Slowking survives, but Rest gives Ampharos initiative and neither is the preservation route.

Existing trajectory rows identify switch Kingdra as better than both attack-now and Dragon Dance. The Outrage-vs-Dragon-Dance pair stays non-strict because hidden Ice coverage makes their internal ordering context-dependent.
Existing trajectory rows identify Swords Dance into Wing Attack as better than both immediate Wing Attack and switching; the remaining attack-vs-switch pair omits that setup route.
Existing labels identify Sand Attack then Gust as the intended probe/progress line; both raw Gust and switching Noctowl omit that best plan.
Existing trajectory rows identify switch Sudowoodo as better than both Focus Punch and Hypnosis into Focus Punch. The missing pair omits the Flying-resist preservation plan.

Existing trajectory rows identify Shadow Ball as better than both Destiny Bond and switching. The missing pair omits the confirmed-KO plan and should not teach sacrifice or preservation over a guaranteed kill.
The taught line is Sleep Powder first, then use the sleep turn for Swords Dance, then attack. Sludge Bomb alone stays active but misses the setup conversion route.
Raw Swords Dance assumes Snorlax cannot punish. The full sleep-then-setup plan creates the setup turn first and still requires re-scoring after a miss or wake.
Sleep Powder then immediate Sludge Bomb is a partial line. The taught policy uses sleep to create the Swords Dance turn before attacking.
Existing teaching says the real plan is Sleep Powder, then Quiver Dance, then Giga Drain. Immediate Giga Drain and raw Quiver Dance both omit that full sequence.
Giga Drain is active damage, but the taught line uses Sleep Powder first to create the Quiver Dance window before attacking.
Raw Quiver Dance assumes Lapras cannot punish. The full sleep-then-setup line creates the setup turn before committing.
Sleep Powder then immediate Giga Drain is only the status half of the line. The taught plan uses the sleep turn to Quiver Dance before attacking.
Raw Quiver Dance and status-then-attack each omit half of the taught sleep/setup conversion route.
Existing feedback says Vaporeon outspeeds and threatens to KO Golem, so Curse before Explosion is too slow. Immediate Explosion at least trades or forces a clean next position.

Saved pairwise feedback explicitly says Explosion and switching to Omastar are both close/live: Explosion trades low-future-value Golem for a clean entry, while Omastar is the Water-resist preservation line.

Curse before Explosion gives the faster Water attacker the punish turn. If not cashing Explosion immediately, the existing feedback prefers preserving the route through Omastar rather than setting up first.

Fixture public notes say revealed Flame Wheel is chip, not a reason to panic-switch; Rock Slide has the stronger route and KO pressure, while Skarmory over-preserves unless stronger Fire coverage is confirmed.
Revealed Flamethrower is a guaranteed KO into Ariados, so the Tentacruel pivot preserves the hazard/status piece while Toxic spends the turn before any poison route can convert.
Leech Life is only about 14-17% into Typhlosion and does not alter the faster guaranteed Flamethrower branch; preserving through Tentacruel is the better transition.
Scyther is faster and public Geodude damage does not cleanly remove it, while Wing Attack is only about 24-29%; Swords Dance uses the safe turn to change the route instead of taking low-value chip.
Switching preserves Scyther only in a shallow sense; the current state is the rare safe setup window, so leaving gives the player a re-score before Bugsy's ace has converted anything.
Umbreon is the clean public Psychic answer because romhack Psychic has no effect on Dark; Hypnosis can work if it hits, but it gambles Poliwrath into faster Psychic while the pivot exists.
Surf is only about 33-39% into Alakazam and Recover is revealed, while Psychic chunks Poliwrath before it moves; switching to Umbreon preserves the ace and denies the public route.
Night Shade is reliable fixed damage, but with Sleep Clause free and Noctowl unstatused, Hypnosis is the route-creating control move before Noctowl keeps playing Peck or Reflect.
Curse spends Haunter HP and starts a slower route while legal sleep can neutralize the current Noctowl immediately; use sleep first, then re-score hit/miss/switch.
Switching to Gengar exposes the ace before the opener is spent. Haunter still has a legal sleep chance and survives the public Peck branch, so Hypnosis is the better transition.

Bayesian/canonical scope companion to the iter-5 pairwise label on this fixture. Public_only scope (the legacy trajectory label at trajectory_preferences.jsonl line 19) preferred attack_now Thunder under strict revealed-moves reasoning. Public_plus_common_meta scope (this label) prefers switch_preserve_then_rescore: Suicune showing only Surf cannot meaningfully touch Dragonair (Dragon resists Surf 0.5x); the player keeping Suicune active is public Bayesian evidence that Suicune carries coverage Ice Beam threatens Dragonair (~70% prior). Switching to Kingdra (Water/Dragon) reduces incoming Ice from 2x to 1x AND adds Water 0.5x resist on Surf. Move_thunder hits SE but eats Ice Beam first. Per user 2026-05-16 direction the Bayesian scope is canonical for this loop; this label is the canonical-scope ground truth and the legacy label is preserved for retrieval/diffing only.
